image_prompt: SVG: line graph titled 'Number of reported cases of bat rabies in Europe'. X-axis 'Year' from 1975 to 2015 (ticks every 5). Y-axis 'Number of infected bats' from 0 to 140 (ticks every 20). Four data points at (1980,100), (1990,25), (2000,10), (2010,~1) with a smooth exponential-decay best-fit curve passing through them.

topic: DNA double helix with base pairs
image_prompt: SVG: vertical DNA double-helix with two sugar-phosphate backbones; A and G bases shown on the left strand; empty dashed target boxes opposite for the student to drop the complementary bases; right-side panel with draggable items T (blue), C (orange), U (red), N (teal); labels 'Complementary base pairs' and 'Sugar-phosphate backbone'.

SVG: empty 2x2 Punnett square with Father (A, a) along the top header and Mother (A, a) on the left header; alongside, side-by-side cross-section comparison of alveoli in a healthy person (clear open lumens, red lungs) vs a person with cystic fibrosis (alveoli filled with yellow-brown mucus), each with a body silhouette and a bronchiole leading to a cluster of alveoli; 'Mucus' label on the CF side.

SVG: four body-silhouette diagrams side by side showing (1) circulatory system with heart and red arteries/blue veins, (2) digestive system with stomach and intestines, (3) nervous system with brain, spinal cord and yellow nerve branches, (4) musculoskeletal/skeleton; each labelled Image 1, Image 2, Image 3, Image 4.

SVG: two skin cross-sections side-by-side labelled Image A and Image B; both show epidermis, dermis with capillary loops and a sweat gland, and a hair follicle; Image A (cold) shows hair standing upright with a goosebump; Image B (hot) shows hair lying flat and sweat droplets on the surface.

SVG: negative-feedback flowchart for body temperature. Central top: 'Body temperature set at 37°C'. Left branch: Environmental change -> 'Body temperature rises above 37°C' -> Body response -> back to centre 'Body temperature returns to 37°C'. Right branch mirrors: Environmental change -> 'drops below 37°C' -> Body response -> 'returns to 37°C'. Orange boxes for env/response, grey boxes for states, arrows linking.

SVG: three sequential microscopy-style frames showing yeast reproducing by budding — Frame 1: a single round yeast cell; Frame 2: cell with a smaller bud emerging; Frame 3: a chain of three cells after division. Grey textured background mimicking microscope.

SVG: top half = draggable items panel (Energy, Oxygen, Sugar, Water as green tiles) above an empty word-equation with four blank slots and the fixed 'Carbon dioxide' tile in the products side; bottom half = three numbered steps with apparatus — Step 1 pouring yeast into a flask, Step 2 flask placed inside a UV chamber, Step 3 flask removed from the chamber.

SVG: five labelled apparatus panels — Step 4 (pipetting 10 cm³ of 5% glucose into a conical flask beside a labelled bottle); Step 5 (flask sealed with rubber stopper and CO2 sensor probe wired to a green-screen meter); Step 6 (sealed flask placed in a 37°C water bath); Step 7 (timer reading 03:00, sensor showing 2000 ppm); Step 8 (refresh/repeat cycle with fresh flask).

Data table: rows for exposure time 0, 30, 45 minutes; columns Student 1-4 CO2 concentration in ppm (2201, 2499, 2397, 2303 / 1278, 1199, 1406, 1321 / 789, 746, 700, 765). Header spanning 'Concentration of CO2 after 3 minutes / ppm'.

topic: Transformed data table with blanks + graph grid
image_prompt: SVG: transformed-data table with three rows (0/2350/783; 30/blank/blank; 45/750/250) for student to fill; plus an empty graph grid titled 'The effect of UV radiation on the rate of respiration of yeast' with Y-axis ticks 400-900 (every 100) and minor gridlines, and a draggable points panel showing three blue diamonds.

topic: Enzyme pathway flow (Sugar -> A -> B -> C)
image_prompt: SVG: horizontal enzyme pathway — Sugar -> Intermediate A -> Intermediate B -> Intermediate C, with E_A, E_B, E_C labels above each arrow and vertical CO2 arrows dropping below each step; legend lines explaining that E_A, E_B, E_C are produced from Gene A, B, C respectively.

topic: Gene DNA changes table + raw CO2 table
image_prompt: SVG: two stacked data tables. Top: 'Number of observed changes in yeast DNA' for Gene A/B/C at exposure times 0,30,45 (0,0,0 / 2,2,2 / 5,3,2). Bottom: repeated raw CO2 concentration table (Student 1-4) identical to page 12.

SVG: food web with top predators (Bear, Cougar, Eagle), mid-level animals (Salmon, Shrew, Raccoon, Frog, Woodpecker, Squirrel), and producers/primary consumers (Deer, Insects and crustaceans, Plants/tree). Arrows show energy flow following the original feeding relationships.

SVG: prompt sentence 'Using the diagram, identify a food chain containing organisms that cannot be infected with rabies.' followed by a row of four empty pink rectangles linked left-to-right by arrows.

SVG: comic-style 3-panel illustration. Left panel: a female vet in pink scrubs with blue gloves vaccinating a small brown-and-white dog on a clinic table, green medical cross on the wall. Top-right panel: two stray dogs (one dark, one tan) foraging around an overflowing trash can. Bottom-right panel: the same two dogs eating from a food bowl marked with a green cross (oral vaccine in food).
